segments.integrated_caption: The draft phase of a professional League of Legends match between Cloud9 and FlyQuest unfolds with an electric atmosphere, vibrant green and blue stage lighting illuminating large screens displaying character art and team logos. Enthusiastic fans in the audience hold up handmade signs, including one for 'BESERKER PLAY' and another for 'VARVEX', their palpable energy contributing to the background hum of the crowd, which occasionally erupts into cheers. On screen, the draft interface shows C9 picking Fiddlesticks and Jhin, while FLY selects Katarina and Jinx. Commentators analyze the strategic implications, discussing how 'Wippo' might be counterpicked, especially with FlyQuest starting on red side, and referencing 'Broken Blade' and the 'mobility with the healing' of certain champions. Close-ups reveal a C9 player with glasses and a teal jersey intensely focused, alongside a contemplative FlyQuest player in a black jacket and headset. The commentators further speculate on 'Fudge's' potential picks, noting his previous discussions about difficulties and the advantage of red side against 'Wippo' for a normal matchup. They express curiosity about what strategies have been 'cooked up for this game one,' mentioning 'Rexxar' being banned out and 'Wippo' having many options. The discussion continues, highlighting 'Olaf' as a powerful pick and noting Cloud9's improved performance in playoffs, all while the camera alternates between the players' focused expressions and the animated crowd, underscoring the tension and excitement of this crucial draft.

segments.integrated_caption: The minute opens with the intense Ban Phase 2 of a League of Legends match between Cloud9 and FlyQuest, set in a vibrant esports arena bathed in green and blue stage lighting. Commentators are heard analyzing the draft, with one describing a pick as "pretty much the most basic standard Evergreeen top laner there can be," while another finds it "pretty interesting," speculating about a "surprise swap around" from FlyQuest. The camera shows a C9 player with curly hair, wearing a teal jersey and headset, intensely focused and gesturing during the draft. The commentators continue to dissect the strategy, discussing locking in "both your solo laners" and "Senna going bot," noting that not getting a counter-pick on red side "can be somewhat of a failed draft." Visually, a FlyQuest player in a black jacket and headset stands observing, while a C9 player with glasses and a teal jersey is deeply concentrated in his Secretlab chair. Another FlyQuest player with long dark hair, wearing a black hoodie, appears thoughtful, resting his chin on his hand. The laughter is explained as a reference to a "dive bet" where analysts Azael and Medios bet on Cloud9 winning the entire split, while another bet on FlyQuest. Throughout this, the on-screen draft interface consistently displays team bans and picks, with the timer counting down from 24 to 12 seconds. The in-game camera closely follows this mid-lane skirmish, culminating in Berserker securing a kill on Massu and leveling up to 2. Simultaneously, C9 Vulcan gains an early advantage trading blows with FLY Busio in the bottom lane, with commentators observing C9's aggressive intent, noting 'C9 really looking to put that aggro immediately into play bottom side' and that 'Busio gets chunked a little bit there by Berserker and Vulcan.' The bottom of the screen displays live player cam feeds, showing intensely focused players like C9 Jojopyun (wearing glasses and a teal jersey), C9 Berserker, C9 Vulcan (with a beard and glasses in black and blue), FLY Jensen (in a black jacket), FLY Massu (with long dark hair in a black hoodie), and FLY Busio, all wearing team jerseys and headsets. The HUD shows a 0-0 score, 2.5K-3.2K gold for both teams, and the minimap reveals team positions, all against a backdrop of sponsor logos like AT&T and PAGODA, creating a tense and competitive atmosphere as the scoreboard updates in real time.

segments.integrated_caption: The video segment captures an intense early-game phase of a League of Legends match between Cloud9 and FlyQuest, set in a dark, forested top lane and river environment. The game timer progresses from 3:10 to 4:13, with the score remaining 0-0 and C9 slightly ahead in gold (5.0K to 4.8K). Two male commentators provide a strategic overview, discussing jungle progression and the advantages of a 'long sword' clear, noting that it's only beneficial if you can 'make something happen with the long sword,' otherwise it's just a 'slower six-camp clear.' They emphasize the importance of combat power for potential early fights. Visually, C9 Jojopyun, a player with glasses in a teal jersey, is seen intensely focused in his player cam feed while engaging in a top lane skirmish near his turret against FLY Jensen and FLY Inspired. C9 Vulcan, with a beard and glasses, and C9 Berserker are also nearby, their focused expressions visible in their player cams. The commentators predict early engagements, stating, 'we're going to end up fighting over something,' whether it's a crab or bottom lane. The action shifts as FLY Inspired attempts to secure a kill near the river, but is interrupted by Jojopyun’s aggressive play. FLY Massu, a player with long dark hair, and FLY Busio are seen rotating into the top lane, their focused player cam feeds reflecting their strategic movements. The minimap reveals coordinated team positioning for early objectives. The commentators note that 'Inspired now pathing back up towards the top side of things,' while 'Blaber has already secured the bottom river scuttle crab,' which means the 'long sword doesn't end up getting to make a whole lot of impact just yet.' They observe that Inspired 'is not going to get anything' from his current play, having been seen by minions. Jojopyun is also noted for placing a ward. The segment concludes with commentators discussing trading scuttles, as the broadcast overlays display real-time stats, player levels, and gold counts, alongside in-game chat messages like 'gl hf' and 'Senna - Alive', maintaining a tense and competitive atmosphere.

segments.integrated_caption: The video segment opens with a live League of Legends match at 12:15, showcasing a tense team fight between Cloud9 (C9) and FlyQuest (FLY) near the Chemtech Drake. Commentators immediately highlight C9's successful steal of the Drake, calling it a "great start for Cloud9" and noting the dragon was down to a critical "38" health. They discuss the timing of the smite, questioning if the enemy jungler had their summoner spell ready, referencing the dragon's health at "400 health, 300 health" and concluding the opponent "did not have it" when they were hitting it. On-screen chat messages like 'LETS GO BWIPO' appear, a sentiment echoed by the commentators who mention "Bwipo" in relation to the Drake steal. A replay then highlights C9 Berserker securing a kill on FLY Inspired with a burst of damage, with C9 Vulcan and C9 Jojopyun also joining the chaotic skirmish. The scene transitions to live gameplay at 13:00, showing C9 players rotating to the top lane where FLY Busio and FLY Inspired engage C9 Jojopyun, resulting in Jojopyun’s death, which prompts an exclamatory "Jojo!" from a commentator. Player cam feeds display C9 Fudge channeling abilities, FLY Busio reacting to the fight, and FLY Inspired being eliminated, while C9 Blaber and FLY Jensen are also visible, focused on the action. The commentators acknowledge an "oopsie there from Cloud9" but confirm C9 is "still up in gold," while FlyQuest has a "nice end towards a potential Soul here," specifically an "Ocean Soul," which they describe as "really powerful." The minimap and HUD continuously provide real-time updates on gold, kills, and positions as the game timer advances to 13:12, all set against the consistent Summoner’s Rift environment under standard LCS broadcast lighting and graphics.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with intense League of Legends gameplay at 13:15, capturing a tense top-lane skirmish between Cloud9 and FlyQuest. Commentators describe the action, noting a "nice play from Inspired kicking him right back into the waiting arm of Busio and Jensen," as FlyQuest players—FLY Busio, FLY Inspired, and FLY Massu—push the top lane, engaging C9’s Fudge and Blaber near the river. C9’s Jojopyun and Berserker rotate in from mid and jungle, respectively, to assist, with C9 Vulcan joining the fray. Commentators highlight that "Busio is kind of getting online, he's got his malignant stun here already," which aligns with FLY Busio initiating a stun on C9 Berserker. The fight erupts near the river’s edge, with C9 retaliating aggressively. C9 Jojopyun lands a critical ability, and C9 Berserker secures a kill on FLY Inspired, who is eliminated after being caught out, with commentators mentioning "Eclipse done for Inspired." Commentators also note that "Jojo" (C9 Jojopyun) is "the target of quite a lot of attention," with FlyQuest "executing well and being able to punish Cloud9 midlaner." The skirmish continues with C9 Fudge and Blaber trading with FLY Massu and Busio, while C9 Vulcan provides support from the backline. The fight shifts slightly toward the river, with C9 gaining the upper hand. The minimap shows FLY Jensen rotating from mid, but he arrives too late. Player cam feeds show intense focus from C9 Jojopyun, C9 Berserker, C9 Vulcan, FLY Jensen, FLY Massu, and FLY Busio, with visible reactions to the high-stakes combat. The HUD displays C9 leading 4-3 in kills, with a minimal gold advantage (0.7K–0.8K). Commentators then turn their attention to C9 Blaber, asking "let's see what Blaber can respond with," noting "he's got his Thunder Sky done, he has his ultimate ready," as they discuss potential Rift Herald plays. On-screen text like 'GIVES YOU WIINGS.' from a Red Bull ad and the 'SECRET LAB' logo appear briefly amidst the dynamic combat animations and continuous game interface updates, all underscored by the sounds of abilities and combat.

C9 Berserker, in his black jersey player cam, follows up with a decisive kill on FLY Inspired, whose visible frustration is mirrored by commentators noting he is "trapped back." FLY Massu, in a black hoodie, rotates in but is eliminated. Commentators also note FLY Bwipo is at half health and C9 Fudge might drop "spiraling despair" as he channels abilities. The commentators express excitement and tension throughout the fast-paced engagement.

segments.integrated_caption: The video segment captures a pivotal team fight unfolding in the top lane, near the river. Visually, C9 Fudge is seen channeling an ability while C9 Vulcan initiates the skirmish by hooking FLY Bwipo. FLY Massu rotates in to support, but C9 Jojopyun uses his ultimate to secure a kill on FLY Inspired, whose frustration is visibly captured in his player cam. C9 Berserker follows up with damage, and C9 Blaber and C9 Jojopyun push forward, destroying the red turret and gaining a gold advantage. FLY Jensen manages to survive the engagement, retreating to lane, while FLY Busio and FLY Massu are eliminated. The camera follows C9 Jojopyun as he levels up to 12 and continues pushing, while FLY Jensen and FLY Inspired retreat. Player cam feeds show intense focus from all participants, with C9 Vulcan and FLY Jensen displaying calm concentration, and FLY Massu showing a moment of disappointment. The minimap reveals coordinated movements, and the game interface updates with gold, kill, and score changes, all set against dynamic green and blue stage lighting. Simultaneously, commentators discuss the broader game state, noting that FlyQuest has just secured a dragon objective, making it "three straight dragons for FlyQuest," and expressing concern for Cloud9's overall position. They highlight FlyQuest's play, mentioning that FLY Jensen "found the seismic shove" and "poked out Berserker so much" that he was "still pretty low" despite picking up honeyfruit. The commentators also recall a previous engagement where FLY Inspired "flashed away from Blaber ulting him" near the dragon pit. They then explain C9's decision to back off a dragon due to "too much poke damage," with C9 Fudge having been "pushed out to like 50 HP maybe," and how C9 secured a secondary mid turret as a "consolation prize." This commentary provides a strategic backdrop, contrasting C9's immediate top lane success with their broader challenges in the game.

segments.integrated_caption: At 20:12, a tense top-lane skirmish unfolds in a League of Legends match between Cloud9 and FlyQuest, with C9 holding a 4-3 kill lead and a 1.6K gold advantage. C9 Vulcan, a bearded player in a black jersey with blue accents, initiates by engaging FLY Bwipo near the top turret. C9 Blaber and C9 Berserker, both in black and blue jerseys, rotate in to support, while C9 Jojopyun, wearing glasses and a teal jersey, positions for his ultimate. FlyQuest attempts to counter with FLY Inspired and FLY Massu, but C9's coordinated pressure forces the fight. C9 Jojopyun lands his ultimate on FLY Inspired, securing a kill, followed by C9 Berserker's quick execution. FLY Massu is eliminated shortly after, while FLY Jensen, in a black jacket, survives but is pushed back. The skirmish concludes with C9 gaining control of the top lane, increasing their gold advantage to 2.0K by 21:00. Throughout the segment, the in-game HUD displays real-time stats, the minimap shows team positions, and scoreboards update. Player cam feeds capture C9 Jojopyun reacting to the kill and FLY Inspired showing frustration. Sponsor logos like Samsung SSD, KIA, and PAGODA are visible, alongside on-screen chat text 'LETS GO BWIPO'. Meanwhile, commentators provide strategic analysis, discussing the explosiveness of champion combos like Gragas, Vi, and Taliyah, and FlyQuest's need to control poke damage or secure another dragon for overall game control. They praise FlyQuest's strong positioning, noting how they were 'so well spread out' to counter potential Cloud9 engages, even when C9 Blaber went in, and how they 'zoned off the follow-up' from a Vi ultimate. Later, the commentators observe FlyQuest playing 'really safe' around 'Jensen flash timers,' highlighting that FLY Jensen 'still has not died' in the game.

The fight intensifies as C9 Jojopyun uses his ultimate to eliminate FLY Inspired, described by the commentator as someone who "lands the stun and deletes that midlaner." C9 Berserker follows up with a kill on FLY Massu, while FLY Jensen survives and retreats. Commentary further analyzes the play, noting "intelligent pathing there from Wippo" (FLY Bwipo) and how "Jojo gets completely caught off guard." The camera then transitions to live gameplay, showing the teams split across the map with C9 pushing top lane and FLY rotating to defend. C9 Vulcan engages FLY Bwipo near the top turret, using a hook to initiate, while C9 Berserker and C9 Jojopyun provide support. C9 Fudge channels abilities from the river, and FLY Busio attempts to rejoin the skirmish. The minimap and scoreboards show a tight 4-4 kill count and a small gold advantage for C9. Player cam feeds display focused expressions: C9 Vulcan and C9 Berserker remain intense, while FLY Jensen and FLY Massu show concentration and slight frustration. Commentary updates on C9 Jojopyun's performance, stating he "is now 0-3 and 0 on the Taliyah this game," and discusses "the walls" in relation to placements, but notes it's "definitely not the performance that you'd want to see." Sponsor logos for AT&T and PAGODA are visible on screen, with AT&T banners near the river and PAGODA in the bottom-left corner, all set against a dark, atmospheric environment with glowing ability effects and the river’s purple aura, enhanced by the broadcast interface's real-time stats and dynamic minimap.

segments.integrated_caption: As the minute begins, two male commentators are heard discussing game mechanics, specifically the devastating 'Severing Bolt' damage, noting it can deal 838 damage if a target is isolated or locked down, even before item procs and runes. Visually, a pivotal team fight erupts in the top lane of a League of Legends match between Cloud9 (C9) and FlyQuest (FLY), with the score tied at 4-4 and C9 holding a 2.2K gold advantage. C9 Jojopyun (Zed) and C9 Blaber (Yone) position near the river as FLY Jensen (Sylas) and FLY Inspired (Katarina) push forward. C9 Vulcan (Nautilus) initiates the skirmish by hooking FLY Bwipo (Darius). The commentators then shift their focus, remarking on C9 Blaber's and C9 Berserker's gold, noting they are 'looking pretty rich' on Cloud9. A full-screen graphic overlay highlights C9 Blaber's milestone of passing XMITHIE for 17th most all-time LCS kills with 972 kills. C9 Jojopyun follows up with a precise ultimate, securing a kill on FLY Inspired, who is then eliminated by C9 Berserker (Aatrox) after being caught out. The commentators observe FLY Inspired 'looking for one of these side lane plays' just before his elimination. C9 Fudge (Garen) channels his ultimate near the top lane turret, while FLY Bwipo attempts to reposition under pressure from C9’s frontline. C9 Jojopyun and C9 Berserker continue their dominance, pushing into FLY’s territory and destroying the red turret. FLY Massu (Yasuo) rotates in but is quickly eliminated in the chaos. The commentators conclude by noting that C9 Berserker 'has a big bounty on his head too,' reflecting his strong performance. The skirmish ends with C9 securing a 2-1 kill exchange and pushing further into FLY’s base, with C9 Fudge and C9 Blaber advancing toward the next objective. Player cam feeds show intense focus from all participants, including C9 Jojopyun and FLY Jensen reacting to the fight’s progression. The minimap reveals coordinated movements, and the game interface displays dynamic gold and kill updates, alongside sponsor logos for AT&T near the river and PAGODA in the bottom left corner.

segments.integrated_caption: The intense League of Legends match between Cloud9 and FlyQuest sees a pivotal top lane team fight unfold with the score tied at 4-4 and C9 holding a 2.4K gold advantage. The action ignites near the river as C9 Jojopyun, focused and aggressive, channels an ability alongside C9 Berserker, engaging FLY Inspired and FLY Massu. Simultaneously, C9 Vulcan, with a beard and glasses, hooks FLY Bwipo near the top lane turret, initiating a broader skirmish. Casters highlight the aggression, with one noting "Blabber getting pretty aggro" as C9 works to steal the enemy blue buff. FLY Jensen, composed in his player cam, and FLY Busio rotate in to support their team. The fight escalates rapidly; casters exclaim "Blabber going in for the Vault Breaker flash! They'll burst down Inspired! They'll take him out! And Berserker's on a rampage!" as FLY Inspired is stunned and killed by C9 Berserker, increasing C9’s kill count to 5. Caster 2 adds, "Nicely done there by Cloud9," and later observes "Inspired playing with fire, he was dancing so close... gets caught by the Q flash from Blabber." FLY Massu is eliminated, showing frustration in his player cam, while C9 Jojopyun, still focused, uses his ultimate effectively in the chaotic engagement. C9 Vulcan and C9 Blaber push forward, and C9 proceeds to destroy the blue turret, confirmed by an on-screen notification. The camera closely follows the action, with the minimap showing player positioning and the game interface displaying dynamic gold counts and kill stats. Casters discuss C9's advantage with the jungler dead, suggesting they might go for Baron, and note that "Jensen also just cleared the top tower while that pick was happening," pushing the wave for "Jojo" to get "very rich." Sponsor logos for AT&T and PAGODA are visible throughout the tense and competitive atmosphere.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with commentators discussing strategic plays, noting the potential for tower gold for FLY Jensen and the inherent danger of the Baron pit. They critique a "wasted flash" from C9 Blaber, arguing that 300 gold isn't worth a flash when "Soul is on the table," deeming it problematic without a follow-up objective. The commentators then praise FlyQuest's calmness, observing FLY Jensen continuing to split push top, securing an objective, and despite C9 giving up gold in the jungle, FLY secured an outer tower. Visually, the game interface displays C9 holding a 2.6K gold lead and a 5-4 kill advantage, with sponsor logos like AT&T and PAGODA visible. The commentators highlight FLY Jensen's growing power, noting he has "two needlessly large rods" and is looking for his "death cap," emphasizing his "three items power spike" will be massive. They also mention C9 Berserker now has his Terminus, giving him his three items. The action intensifies as a pivotal team fight erupts in the top lane around the 27:00 mark. C9 Jojopyun, seen focused in his player cam, uses his ultimate to secure a kill on FLY Inspired, who reacts with visible frustration in his player cam. C9 Berserker, near the top lane turret, also executes a decisive kill on FLY Inspired. C9 Vulcan, with a beard and glasses, engages FLY Bwipo with a hook, initiating the skirmish. Commentators describe this, saying "Whippos gonna get jumped on here at the very start," while C9 Fudge, seen channeling his ultimate, is "stunned up," and his "cast goes out but it doesn't get much," leaving him at "about half HP," with "Whippos even lower." FLY Massu, shown in his player cam, rotates in from mid lane but is quickly eliminated by C9’s coordinated frontline. C9 Fudge channels his ultimate, dealing massive area damage, while FLY Busio attempts to reposition but is caught in the crossfire. C9 Blaber and C9 Jojopyun maintain pressure, securing multiple kills. Player cam feeds show intense focus from all participants, with C9 Vulcan and FLY Massu visibly reacting to the chaos, all set against dynamic lighting and stage visuals with sponsor banners.

segments.integrated_caption: The video segment captures an intense team fight unfolding in the top river area of the League of Legends map, centered around the Dragon pit, with dynamic visual effects from abilities and the League of Legends HUD displaying real-time stats. The battle ignites as FLY Bwipo initiates by engaging C9 Vulcan with a hook, prompting an excited commentator to exclaim, "a big kick ends up hitting free!" and "Vulcan, pop first." The skirmish quickly escalates, with C9 Jojopyun, seen focused in his player cam, using his ultimate to secure a kill on FLY Inspired, who is then followed by a 'DOUBLE KILL' for C9 Berserker, eliminating FLY Massu. Despite these C9 successes, a commentator states, "Jensen goes on a killing spree" and "Jensen's painting their blood all over the floor," while also announcing, "Shut down over to Busio, as Berserker falls," which contrasts with the visual of C9 Berserker's double kill. FLY Jensen, visible in his player cam, survives the initial onslaught and retreats, while C9 Fudge channels abilities to support his team, with a commentator remarking, "Fudge tries to run, but the big man ain't going nowhere." The rapid-fire commentary continues with "Shut down over to Inspired, solo over to FlyQuest," followed by a chant, "Fight for FlyQuest!" As C9 gains momentum, securing multiple kills and pushing into the Dragon pit, the atmosphere remains tense. A second commentator praises FlyQuest's "beautiful spacing out Berserker, not allowing him into the fight," and analyzes their "massive" range advantage, predicting they'll get "all the kills, plus the soul, plus the Baron," and "wrapping up the game right there." Sponsor logos for AT&T and PAGODA are visible on screen. The fight culminates with the Red team slaying the Dragon, confirmed by an on-screen notification and the Dragon’s summoning animation. A third commentator, Flowers, adds, "Absolutely everything except Jojo," noting that C9 Jojopyun has been killed multiple times, but they're "not even too worried about that," as "Baron about to drop." Throughout, player cams show intense reactions from C9 Vulcan, C9 Jojopyun, C9 Berserker, FLY Jensen, FLY Massu, and FLY Busio, reflecting the fast-paced combat.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with the pivotal moment of FLY Jensen securing Baron Nashor, triggering a 'Baron Power Play' notification, as commentators remark on Cloud9's lost gold lead and FlyQuest "now calling the shots" with the "pendulum swings hard." The scene then shifts to a chaotic replay of a team fight near the river, where FLY Bwipo, identified by commentators as "Wippo" who "survives on the Renekton" despite being initially targeted, initiates by engaging C9 Vulcan near the top lane turret. The fight erupts with FLY Jensen and FLY Inspired diving in, while C9 Jojopyun and C9 Berserker respond aggressively. Commentators note "Berserker is just having so much trouble actually getting in an auto-wing" and "Wippo starts backing off" as "poke starts landing on him," including "Busio poke being thrown at him." C9 Vulcan is quickly eliminated by FLY Jensen, followed by a double kill for FLY Jensen as he takes down C9 Berserker, accompanied by distinct in-game sounds and ability effects. The fight intensifies with FLY Massu and FLY Busio joining, but C9 Jojopyun lands a crucial ultimate, securing a kill on FLY Inspired, who is shown in his player cam feed reacting with visible frustration. Visual effects like 'CHANNELING', 'STUNNED', and 'AIRBORNE' flash across the screen, enhancing the dynamic abilities and positioning. The fight concludes with FLY Jensen surviving and leveling up, while C9’s frontline is decimated. Commentators praise FlyQuest for playing "so well, so patient" through the minutes Cloud9 had a big early lead, and discuss their "range advantage with an Ocean Soul." The broadcast cuts to a focused and composed player cam of FLY Jensen before gameplay resumes with FLY pushing the top lane, destroying a blue turret. The scoreboard shows FLY leading 8-5 with a 2.5K gold advantage, while on-screen chat messages like 'INSPIRED IS TOO GOOD MAN' and 'LETS GO FLYQUEST!' reflect the audience’s excitement. The vibrant environment, complete with the game’s HUD, minimap, sponsor logos like Samsung SSD, KIA, and Red Bull, and dynamic green and blue stage lighting, underscores the competitive atmosphere.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with commentators discussing FlyQuest's strategic play with the Baron buff and Lee Sin's flexibility, setting the stage for an impending clash. Around the 30-minute mark, an intense team fight erupts in the top lane as FLY Bwipo initiates on C9 Vulcan near the blue turret, which is swiftly destroyed. The commentators exclaim, "FlyQuest have really built this lead up now as they try to get the engage on to Bwipo!" as the action unfolds. C9 Fudge, positioned near the river, channels his ultimate to support his team. The fight escalates rapidly; C9 Jojopyun uses his ultimate to secure a kill on FLY Inspired, with commentators noting, "Inspired with a nice kick back on to Jojo but it ain't gonna matter, Inspired drops next!" C9 Berserker follows up with a powerful burst, eliminating FLY Massu and achieving a killing spree, prompting the commentators to declare, "C9 just got three and they're about to make it four!" and later, "Massu tries to kite him out, but Blaber and Berserker are in hot pursuit." The commentators also mentioned earlier that "Berserker's already got the first kill on the Croc," highlighting his early impact. C9 Vulcan, despite being rooted, survives and continues to engage, while C9 Blaber provides support from the backline. FLY Jensen and FLY Busio attempt to reposition but are pressured by C9’s frontline, with Busio being targeted by C9. The commentators excitedly conclude, "You wanted to see a Kalista fight? There's your Kalista fight!" as the fight ends. C9 secures multiple kills, including FLY Inspired and FLY Massu, while FLY Bwipo and FLY Jensen survive but are forced to retreat. The game interface shows C9 with a slight gold advantage, and the minimap reveals coordinated team movements. Player cam feeds display focused expressions from C9 Jojopyun, C9 Berserker, C9 Vulcan, FLY Jensen, FLY Massu, and FLY Busio, with visible reactions to the fight’s progression. Sponsor logos for Samsung SSD, KIA, and Red Bull are displayed, and the broadcast includes real-time chat messages like 'INSPIRED IS TOO GOOD MAN' and 'LETS GO FLYQUEST!', adding to the competitive atmosphere.

segments.integrated_caption: The intense League of Legends LCS match between Cloud9 and FlyQuest unfolds in the top lane, with C9 pushing aggressively towards the inhibitor. Commentators excitedly narrate C9's attempt to tank for the inhibitor, noting they are 'rushing down mid' and want to 'get this inhibitor.' As C9 Fudge, C9 Blaber, C9 Jojopyun, C9 Berserker, and C9 Vulcan coordinate to destroy the turret and inhibitor, FLY Jensen, FLY Massu, FLY Busio, and FLY Bwipo engage defensively. The commentators speculate FLY Jensen might 'snipe it out with a severing bolt,' which 'would mean a lot here.' The fight erupts near the inhibitor, with C9 Berserker securing a kill on FLY Inspired, whose frustration is visible in the player cam. Amidst the chaos, commentators exclaim 'Protect the president!' and 'Get down Mr. Cannon!' with laughter, as C9 Jojopyun uses his ultimate to eliminate FLY Inspired, and C9 Vulcan engages FLY Bwipo with a hook. C9 Fudge channels his ultimate, and C9 Blaber supports from the backline, leading to the inhibitor's destruction and a significant gold advantage for C9. segments.integrated_caption: The intense team fight erupts in the top lane near the river, with commentators setting the scene, noting a "long trip into enemy territory" and "Lee Sin trying to get behind mid lane." C9 Vulcan, visible with his beard and glasses, initiates the skirmish by hooking FLY Bwipo, who is seen in his black hoodie. C9 Fudge, also in his black jersey, is pushed back by the "presence of Inspired" as he channels a powerful ability, trying to create space. The action escalates rapidly as C9 Berserker, focused in his player cam, quickly secures a kill on FLY Inspired, triggering a 'SHUT DOWN!' notification and a 'C9 Berserker is on a rampage!' banner on the game interface. FLY Massu rotates in to support, while FLY Bwipo manages to find C9 Jojopyun, who is then called out by commentators as "Jojo is low!" but then "they're gonna grab him a kill!" as C9 Jojopyun, wearing glasses and a teal jersey, uses his ultimate to eliminate FLY Inspired again, likely after a revive. The commentators exclaim "taken a kill!" as FLY Inspired, seen reacting with frustration in his player cam, is chased down. C9 Fudge then secures another kill on FLY Busio. The fight continues with commentators predicting "Blaber's gonna die next!" and announcing "there goes Vulcan!" as C9 Vulcan is eliminated. C9 Berserker is heard to "fight for his life" in the jungle, narrowly escaping death. FLY Jensen, focused in his player cam, lands a critical root on C9 Fudge, allowing FLY Busio to finish him off, with commentators noting "Fudge gets jumped on!" and that "he'll pay for it with his life." Finally, the commentators announce "As Inspired gets picked off!" as FLY Inspired is eliminated for the final time. The skirmish culminates in C9 destroying the FLY top lane turret, shifting the gold advantage to 1.4K in favor of C9. FlyQuest, still waiting for Inspired to get back, is advised by commentators to 'take this pretty slow.' segments.integrated_caption: The intense late-game team fight between Cloud9 and FlyQuest erupts near the Dragon pit, with commentators noting "Cloud9" and "FlyQuest" as the action unfolds. FlyQuest initiates, securing the Elder Dragon buff, a pivotal moment highlighted by the on-screen notification and the commentator's call of "Elder at half HP." The battle quickly escalates into a chaotic skirmish, filled with the sounds of abilities clashing. C9 Fudge, seen channeling abilities, is noted by commentators to be "about 1/3rd HP." C9 Jojopyun uses his ultimate to secure a kill on FLY Inspired, a play the commentators emphasize by exclaiming, "They've got Jojo, yet again!" FLY Bwipo engages C9 Vulcan with a hook, but C9 Berserker retaliates, eliminating FLY Inspired and contributing to a double kill for C9. segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with intense League of Legends action as Cloud9 (C9) and FlyQuest (FLY) clash in a chaotic team fight near the blue base, with abilities flashing and turrets being destroyed. Another caster highlights, "Jensen goes deathless in this game, playing the mid," and praises FLY Bwipo, stating, "Bwipo's game IQ is just off the charts," elaborating on his intelligent positioning. Visually, FLY players, including FLY Bwipo, FLY Inspired, FLY Jensen, FLY Massu, and FLY Busio, are seen standing up and celebrating their victory as they exit the neon-lit stage area, passing sponsor banners.
